items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution via Improper Output Encoding in Webhooks
items.summary: A critical unauthenticated, zero-interaction vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source webhooks component could lead to arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
items.summary: This critical vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source allows an attacker to upload dangerous file types, potentially leading to privilege escalation or arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) leading to Privilege Escalation
items.summary: A stored XSS flaw in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source enables privilege escalation by injecting scripts into the admin backend, which can lead to admin account takeover.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in the Mirasvit Cache Warmer extension for Magento and Adobe Commerce, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization Vulnerability (Auth Bypass)
items.summary: An incorrect authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to the application.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento REST API
items.summary: Sansec identified 'PolyShell', a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that permits unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.
